claims.claim: Stablecoins & payments — clearest product-market fit, programmable money rails
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Crypto x AI — proof of personhood, provenance, agents transacting, sybil resistance
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: DePIN — token-incentivized physical infrastructure (compute/GPU, storage, wireless, energy, mapping)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Onchain consumer apps with real mainstream usage (e.g. prediction markets)
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Wallet UX & chain abstraction — hiding gas, keys, bridging to make crypto invisible
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Memecoin casino dynamics are extractive/negative-sum and a liability
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: L2 / liquidity fragmentation degrading composability and UX
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Low-float / high-FDV token launches produce poor price discovery and dump on retail
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Risk-on but selective, driven by favorable post-election US regulation and real usage
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Built and heavily promoted a 7-part token taxonomy (network tokens vs company-backed vs arcade vs memecoins, etc.), declaring 'network tokens' the king and the network/company distinction the most underappreciated in crypto — a central new intellectual focus the prediction didn't anticipate
Newly elevated enthusiasm for 'arcade tokens' as 'the most underappreciated token type in crypto,' urging apps to weigh arcade tokens vs stablecoins

Developed a detailed network-token value-accrual / L1 valuation thesis (EIP-1559 burn, blockspace-as-commodity, tokens as equity in 'decentralized network marketplaces,' skepticism of DCF/'ETH is oil')
Major recurring emphasis on ZK proving systems / Jolt / zkVMs (sumcheck, Twist+Shout, Dory) and formal verification (Halmos) as a technical frontier
Pro-open-source-funding stance (backing Merit Systems) and a pro-open-platform, anti-app-store / anti-'design slop' position on protecting the open web
Framed 'identity for AI agents' and proof-of-human (World ID as root of trust) as a top emerging design space, plus a sharper decentralization framing separating 'diffusion' from 'control'
notes: No claims were contradicted — Eddy stayed consistently risk-on (regulatory optimism repeatedly echoed: 'never been a better time to start a project in crypto'), pro-stablecoin (RAI/PMF and stablecoin-growth tweets), pro-Crypto×AI (extensive proof-of-human/World content), and anti-memecoin (memecoins 'just redistribute' value, 'it sucks'). Wallet UX/chain abstraction is confirmed on the thinner basis of the gas-abstraction reply ('no gas token because the app handles everything'). DePIN, mainstream consumer apps/prediction markets, L2 fragmentation, and low-float/high-FDV were simply not addressed in the window (he critiqued pie-chart tokenomics and company-backed tokens, but not the specific low-float/high-FDV unlock pattern). The dominant surprise is how much of the window was spent on token classification, value accrual, and ZK engineering rather than the predicted sector theses.
